title: Veerabhadra Temple
tagline: Veerabhadra Temple — spiritual town of Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: Veerabhadra temple is a Hindu temple located in the Lepakshi, in the state of Andhra Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Veerabhadra temple is a Hindu temple located in the Lepakshi, in the state of Andhra Pradesh, India. The temple is dedicated to Virabhadra, a fierce form of Shiva, a major deity in Hinduism. Built in the 16th century, the architectural features of the temple are in the pallava style and saluva and tuluva Vijayanagara style with profusion of carvings and paintings at almost every exposed surface of the temple. It is one of the centrally protected monuments of national importance and is considered one of the most spectacular Vijayanagara temples.

topPlaces.name: Lepakshi
topPlaces.description: Lepakshi is a village in the Sri Sathya Sai district of Andhra Pradesh, India. It is situated15 km (9.3 miles) eastward of Hindupur, 82 km (51 miles) westward of Kadiri and 120 km (75 mi) north of Bangalore.

topPlaces.name: Nagaragere, Gauribidanur
topPlaces.description: Nagaragere is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Gauribidanur taluk of Chikkaballapura district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Beerepalli, Hindupur mandal
topPlaces.description: Beerepalli is a village in Hindupur mandal, Sri Sathya Sai district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It also serves as the headquarters of M. Beerepalli panchayat.

items.note: Lepakshi is a village in the Sri Sathya Sai district of Andhra Pradesh, India. It is situated15 km (9.3 miles) eastward of Hindupur, 82 km (51 miles) westward of Kadiri and 120 km (75 mi) north of Bangalore.
Nagaragere is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Gauribidanur taluk of Chikkaballapura district in Karnataka.
Beerepalli is a village in Hindupur mandal, Sri Sathya Sai district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It also serves as the headquarters of M. Beerepalli panchayat.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Veerabhadra Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Veerabhadra Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Veerabhadra Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (68 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (92 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Veerabhadra Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Veerabhadra Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Veerabhadra Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Veerabhadra Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Veerabhadra Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Lepakshi, Nagaragere, Gauribidanur, Beerepalli, Hindupur mandal.
